summary: The reproduction bundle clones the vulnerable Rails v7.2.3 source, mounts it read-only into the container, starts a minimal network wrapper around Active Storage DiskService, and verifies out-of-root file read and write through crafted keys.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make a crafted key escape the Active Storage disk root, read /secret.txt, write /owned.txt, and print the success signal EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: network path traversal and arbitrary write reproduced.
actual_observed_result:
Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned rails/rails at the pinned v7.2.3 commit, built the reproduction image, installed the required Ruby dependencies inside the container, and produced READ_BODY="TOP-SECRET
", WRITE_BODY="WRITE_OK
", OWNED_BODY="PWNED
", followed by EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: network path traversal and arbitrary write reproduced.
verification_method: The exploit harness issues real HTTP requests to a localhost server that forwards a key parameter into DiskService. Verification passes only if the response body contains TOP-SECRET, the out-of-root file /owned.txt contains PWNED, and the script emits the exact EVOHUNT_SUCCESS signal.
evidence_produced_by_run: The harness returned READ_BODY="TOP-SECRET\n" after requesting /read?key=../../secret.txt through the localhost HTTP wrapper.
The harness returned WRITE_BODY="WRITE_OK\n" after requesting /write?key=../../owned.txt&content=PWNED through the localhost HTTP wrapper.
The container verified OWNED_BODY="PWNED\n" from /owned.txt outside the configured storage root.
The run emitted the exact success signal EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: network path traversal and arbitrary write reproduced.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
